name: Excessive Nslookup Usage
notes: This rule triggers when the nslookup command is executed an excessive amount of times from the same machine. This behaviour could indicate a malicious actor attempting to gather information about the network to exfiltrate at a later date.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Repeated Nslookup Usage match at least 10 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Repeated Nslookup Usage match at least 10 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Repeated Nslookup Usage match at least 10 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Excessive Nslookup Usage on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Repeated Nslookup Usage match at least 10 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event description: An excessive amount of nslookup command from the same machine has been observed.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
